feedback.text: The digital act is a good start, but it is lacking few key points to protect consumers from current day processes: 1. AI reviews and AI related misrepresentations businesses can do, we should aim to flag AI generated content towards consumer to protect them against such possibilities. AI is enabling companies to create way too easily new surfaces to cause purchase decision to go wrong. 2. Ownership in digital age, we can look at games as an example here. In the last few years what you are buying is turning more and more difficult to understand for consumer. Games keep getting turned off, even the whole marketplaces that are sharing the games get turned off leading to the content vanishing from access. This has not been such a problem with physical media, before, but now its turning into massive issue as we have seen with games becoming more online, leading to even the physical media getting turned into non functioning works. This is causing people whom go to store to possibly buy products that do not work. In addition we are seeing companies protecting their IP's from access of the legally purchased users such as in nintendos case, even when they themselves have closed the sharing network (wii online services as example) and forcing consumers this way to rebuy the purchase in newer hardware. This is leading to consumers getting their right of ownership to get limited. There are hundreds of examples and there is a initative of stop destroying games arriving to EU's process for new games, but the existing market condition is not affecting them at all. 3. Digital services turning into streaming and content getting lost. We are seeing big players from US such as Netflix or Disney turning their products into streaming services and offering us video and serie content through them, but having no way to keep these series visible, as they might be streaming only. This is causing media to become lost as the series are pulled out by the IP owner. This is causing more of the artistic media vanishing from us and consumers being treated unfairly by the IP holder, as there is no change to buy these services at all. This is possible through the digital services enabling turning off and on content. 4. Regionality of content. We are allowing Europe to have separarate licensing agreements in different countries, this is leading to streaming services as in point 2 to be able to treat europeans differently depending on country, leading to consumers having different rights depending on the country of origin.
